county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Excellent preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Guilford 1779. Pre-1779 records in Guilford County (formed from Rowan and Orange 1771). Strong Quaker settlement — New Garden, Deep River, Back Creek meetings. Parent of Davidson (1822), Moore (partly). Naomi Wise murder (1807) — NC's oldest folk murder ballad — occurred here.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1779. Randolph County ROD has excellent online portal. Randolph County Public Library Heritage Room has extensive local collections. Quaker records at Guilford College Friends Historical Collection essential for early families. Randolph County Genealogical Society is very active.

source_profiles.source_name: Randolph County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Excellent online deed search from county formation.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1851.

source_profiles.source_name: Randolph County Quaker Records — Friends Historical Collection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deep River, Back Creek, and other Randolph County Quaker meeting records. Birth, marriage, death, and membership records essential for early families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Anson 1779. Pre-1779 records in Anson County (formed from Bladen 1750). Parent of Scotland (1899) and Robeson (partly). Sandhills region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Richmond County ROD. For pre-1779, use Anson County records. Richmond County Historical Society has local collections.

source_profiles.source_name: Richmond County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Bladen 1787. NC's largest county by area. Home of the Lumbee Tribe — NC's largest Native American group. Significant Scots Gaelic settlement. Pre-1787 records in Bladen County (triple-burned).
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Robeson County ROD online. For pre-1787, Bladen County records (damaged by fires). Lumbee tribal records may be relevant for Native American genealogy. Robeson County Public Library has local history room. NC Commission of Indian Affairs records at NC State Archives.

source_profiles.source_name: Robeson County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1787 and registers from 1851.

source_profiles.source_name: NC Commission of Indian Affairs Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Records related to Lumbee Tribe recognition, land claims, and Indian community records. Essential for Robeson County Native American genealogy.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Guilford 1785. Pre-1785 records in Guilford County. Parent of Caswell (partly). Settled by Scotch-Irish and English families via the Great Wagon Road. Dan River and Smith River valleys.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Rockingham County ROD online. For pre-1785, use Guilford County records. Rockingham County Historical Society and Museum in Wentworth has local collections. DigitalNC has Rockingham newspapers.

source_profiles.source_name: Rockingham County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1785 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Exceptional preservation for a colonial county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Anson 1753. One of NC's most important colonial counties — original Rowan was enormous, stretching to the Blue Ridge. Parent of Surry (1771), Mecklenburg (partly via Tryon), Burke (1777), and many others. Salisbury was the colonial backcountry capital. Great Wagon Road terminus. Scotch-Irish, German, and English settlement. Rowan County Court was the major colonial backcountry court.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1753. Rowan County ROD has excellent online portal from 1753. Rowan Public Library Edith Clark History Room is outstanding. Thyatira Presbyterian Church records (1753+) are among the best church records in the Piedmont. NC State Archives has court minutes from 1753.

source_profiles.source_name: Rowan County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Excellent online deed search from county formation. Colonial-era records.
Colonial-era wills from 1753. Significant for backcountry genealogy.
Marriage bonds from 1753. Colonial bonds significant.
Colonial court minutes from 1753. The most important backcountry court in colonial NC. Apprenticeship bonds, road orders, tavern licenses, and criminal proceedings.

source_profiles.source_name: Rowan Public Library — Edith Clark History Room
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Outstanding local research facility with extensive genealogical collections. Vertical files, family histories, cemetery records, and published abstracts.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1779 when extinct Tryon County was divided — Rutherford (western part) and Lincoln (eastern part). Pre-1779 records in Tryon County papers. Parent of Henderson (1838), McDowell (partly 1842), Polk (partly 1855), Cleveland (partly 1841). Battle of Kings Mountain (1780) nearby — significant Revolutionary War records.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Rutherford County ROD online. For pre-1779, check Tryon County records at NC State Archives. Rutherford County Farm Museum has local history. Revolutionary War pension records relevant for early families.

source_profiles.source_name: Rutherford County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1851.
